lastReleaseMessage: v0.10.0: bar-raise drain (D-006 loopback bind, D-007 import/rollback strict backup-fail, error_log + logEvent, daily prune, regex hoist, analytics surface modules, dedup guard, dashboard preservation, drift-fix F1-F8); .finances/ removed from git index (D-001/D-002 enforcement)

barRaise.topFinding: Fifth consecutive stall, and the first where the uncommitted pile changed character instead of just growing by one file. Head is still e6cac08 (2026-05-29), eight days cold, and the code is healthy (seventh straight zero-drift canonical audit, verified line-by-line against db.ts and server.ts). But overnight a hand-run drift pass widened the working tree from a clean three-doc batch into six modified tracked files (now including CLAUDE.md and two scripts/*.ps1), left two untracked .bak artifacts, added a UTF-8 BOM regression, and triggered a CRLF flip the repo has no .gitattributes to absorb. Nothing committed. Both structural HIGHs carry: the uncommitted tree (maintainability) and the seven uninstalled CLAUDE.md skills that leave the loop with no executor (skill-suite health). The old 'one doc-only commit closes it' advice is stale; the tree is no longer doc-only. Zero of five prior actions closed; the pile is 26 items and compounding.
